threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the buses run on time after 10 PM? Welcome to Clapton, where the 253 is basically a myth after dark.
Caught the N3 last night and overheard a guy bragging about his wild night at the Windmill, but he was actually just talking up a bus stop argument.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street musician playing a tuba outside the Kebap shop at midnight, and everyone just danced around him like it was normal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you hear a techno bass line while waiting for a bus that’s already late—makes every commute feel like part of the nightlife.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively know to double-check BART schedules before making dinner plans—those delays can ruin your whole night.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 7 bus, practicing his speech while clutching a bouquet. Only in the Tenderloin do you see pre-wedding jitters on public transit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars close early. Meanwhile, the locals know where to find the secret late-night spots that stay buzzing till dawn.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their bewildered expression at the Mercat de Sant Antoni when trying to navigate the layout.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when a bus screams past you on Mare Street, like you've never seen traffic move with such chaos. Welcome to Hackney!
Caught a night bus from Brixton, and the driver played old reggae tracks. Even the grumpiest passengers couldn't resist tapping their feet.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One night, I heard a saxophone playing 'Careless Whisper' while a cat strutted down the street like it owned the place. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find spontaneous street parties where the crowd sings along to techno tracks blaring from a random balcony at dawn.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can predict the exact moment BART's going to get stuck again, without even checking the app.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver singing to a group of tourists while waiting at the stop. Even the guy in a suit was cracking up. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just pop into a bar and get a drink immediately. In Gràcia, you’ll often wait for your order while everyone enjoys the conversation first.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle to find the right 'cerveseria'; they ask for 'cerveza' and get a look from the locals.
